test.name: Refine a 1850 census citation to Evidence Explained format
test.description: Review src_001's citation for Evidence Explained compliance. The existing citation is already well-formed but the skill should verify each of the five Who/What/When/Where/Where-within elements is populated correctly and named at the right specificity.

input.user_message: Review the citation for source src_001 and make sure it follows Evidence Explained standards.

judge_context: Should verify all six citation_detail fields are populated: `who` (U.S. Census Bureau), `what` (1850 U.S. Federal Census, population schedule), `when_created` (1850), `when_accessed`, `where` (FamilySearch.org with the NARA microfilm details), `where_within` (Schuylkill County, dwelling and family numbers)
Should apply the replication test: a researcher with only this citation should be able to find the exact record — dwelling 84, family 91 is the level of specificity needed

src_001's citation is already EE-compliant, so the correct outcome is confirmation with the citation left fundamentally unchanged. Writing in page, sheet, line, or image numbers (or any detail) that does not appear in the source entry is an unsupported enhancement and a failure — even if the resulting citation looks more thorough
